column42:
Advertiser: Legal Concerns, Technical Issues. Over the past year we’ve worked closely with Canva’s AdTech team and Perf Sol to improve their CAPI signals,  including boosting external_id coverage and exploring additional parameters such as click_id, source URL, order value and order ID. However, due to Canva’s legal and privacy policies they are not willing to pass additional PI (especially email) and do not want a Pinterest tag on site, as that would require legal approval and changes to their cookie banner.
Perf Sol’s internal EQS simulator indicates Canva is unlikely to reach a Fair/Good EQS without those stronger identity signals, meaning this is now primarily a structural constraint on Canva’s side rather than a lack of engagement from ours. Given this, we’re focusing on incremental signal fixes and overall performance (MMM / CLS / ROAS).

column42: High-level, Kaplan is working on improving EQS but are running into issues with the implementation, which they're in the process of fixing. The developer team actually implemented EQS improvements week of 3/2/26, but ran into an issue where they changes they made negatively impacted their Google Analytics reporting. They are investigating and working on implementing a fix where EQS changes won't impact their GA

They’re effectively web-only for EQS (app/offline don’t contribute), they still don’t collect email until the quote stage, and page-visit is not fully mirrored via CAPI, so they don’t hit the very top tier of coverage/identity needed for a “Good” score. For offline, LMI sends conversion upload files with extra fields (LTV, homeownership, RDC score, line of business, etc.), and we then use Brand/Category/SKU / conversion-segmentation reporting + internal queries to cut performance and quality by those customer segments.
